situational_context: Early rebuild phase with ACWR spike requiring absorption. Gravel ride on Apr 25 (load 57, suffer 57) spiked ACWR to 1.44 against a low chronic base of 22. Training readiness recovered from 25 to 75 in 48h — autonomic system coping well. Chronic load rising (17.8→22 over 8 days), first real progress since extended detraining period. E-bike commuting continues as baseline activity on 'sur site' days (38 km round trip, load ~10-15). Weight trending down nicely: 106.89→104.55 kg over 12 days. Young baby continues to create scheduling variability — skipped sessions are expected and not concerning. HRV stable at 38 ms (7d avg), RHR improved from 53→50 bpm. Strategy: Z1-only until ACWR normalises below 1.3 (expected by May 1-2), then resume Z2 build. Weekend double sessions per user preference.

recommendations.text: Respect ACWR absorption: Z1-only until Wed-Thu, then Z2 from Friday onward. If Tuesday/Thursday gravel commute suffer score >30, cancel the next planned session and add a rest day.
recommendations.measurable: ACWR below 1.3 by May 1; no session above load 15 before Wednesday

recommendations.text: Protein intake consistency: hit ≥150g/day on training days, ≥130g on rest days. Last 7d average was 128g — below target for a 105 kg athlete in rebuild phase.

Continue weight trajectory — current -2 kg/12d pace is sustainable. Maintain caloric awareness without aggressive restriction; the deficit appears to come naturally from activity + logging discipline.
recommendations.measurable: Weight 7d avg ≤105 kg by next week while maintaining training load

markers_to_watch.threshold: Must return to [0.8, 1.3] band within 5-7 days
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 1.44 after gravel spike — elevated injury risk until absorbed

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 35-40 over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: At 22, still only 1.4% of historical peak (1608). Structured sessions + commutes should push this up
Improved from 53 to 50 over 8 days — positive cardiac adaptation signal
